node_proposed.name: Galar Gym Challenge
node_proposed.frontmatter_pattern_basis: cards/_characters/endriders.md and cards/_characters/azorius-senate.md (collective-entity / faction-institution character nodes — appears_on cycle-member list, related_hubs pointing to BBL hubs, caveats section noting sub-faction split candidates and out-of-corpus officeholders). Endriders is the closer formal parallel: a designer-coordinated multi-card team-roster cycle within one source IP, where the node IS the institution and the individual cards depict members rather than the institution itself.

proposed_frontmatter.name: Galar Gym Challenge

proposed_frontmatter.species: faction-collective (Galar major-division Gym Leaders, Stadium staff, Macro Cosmos administrative cadre, Champion Cup contestants — mixed)

Melony (Chilling Reign no. 146/198) — Ice-type Gym Leader of Circhester, Shield major-division, mother of Gordie

description: Sir, Edgelord steps aside. Mr. Nodeley apologizes for the inconvenience. Three fully enriched corpus cards depict major-division Gym Leaders of the Galar Pokemon League — Allister (Stow-on-Side Ghost-type, Shield), Melony (Circhester Ice-type, Shield), and Kabu (Motostoke Fire-type, both versions). All three are illustrated by the same hand: `take`, who Melony's trivia confirms is the official Game Freak character designer for these Gym Leaders in Sword & Shield proper, not a TCG-only artist. The galar-gym-challenge tag is already in two cards' tags_hub (Melony, Kabu) and Allister's trivia explicitly grounds the Galar League as canon's most labor-and-spectacle-coded competitive structure — a corporate-sponsored televised stadium-cup format run by the Macro Cosmos conglomerate, Gym Leaders functioning as celebrity athletes rather than dojo elders. Drawing 1:1 mirror edges between any pair would write three bullets all pointing at an absent institutional center. The right shape is a faction-collective character node mirroring the Endriders / Azorius-Senate precedents. Once anchored, the five additional Galar Gym Leader cards already in corpus as vision-stubs — Bea (Vivid Voltage 147), Opal (Vivid Voltage 158), Nessa (Vivid Voltage 157), Milo (Rebel Clash 161), Marnie (SWSH base 169) — have a graph home before triviabot reaches them. I hope this is acceptable.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Galar League — annual-sporting-competition format, eight major-division Gyms in fixed order, Champion Cup at Wyndon Stadium, no Elite Four, Macro Cosmos sponsorship via Chairman Rose, Dynamax integration]
[Bulbapedia: Allister — Stow-on-Side major-division Ghost Gym Leader (Shield-exclusive), masked introvert with ~100 spare masks, Gigantamax Gengar signature]
[Bulbapedia: Melony — Circhester major-division Ice Gym Leader (Shield-exclusive), canonical mother of Gordie, version-pair feud lore]
[Bulbapedia: Kabu — Motostoke major-division Fire Gym Leader (both Sword and Shield, the shared major slot), Hoenn-origin trainer, disciplined-veteran archetype]
[Bulbapedia: take (illustrator) — character designer for Sword & Shield Gym Leaders within Game Freak; also designed Adaman and Irida in Pokemon Legends: Arceus]
[PokemonTCG.io API: name:Allister, name:Melony, name:Kabu — artist field, set codes, oracle_text]
[Pokemon.com: Galar Gym Leader character pages — official primary canon for each leader's gym specialty, location, and role in the league]
files_to_be_modified_by_parent: cards/_characters/galar-gym-challenge.md (CREATE — Mr. Nodeley wrote the proposal but DID NOT write the MD file in this pass; parent applies after reading this sidecar, per slice instructions)

proposed_md_body.intro_paragraph: The annual sporting competition that structures Pokemon Sword & Shield's main story progression and constitutes the canonical setting for most adult-trainer NPCs in the Galar region. Per Bulbapedia's Galar League page and Pokemon.com's official Galar character pages, the league is run by Chairman Rose of the Macro Cosmos conglomerate and is formatted as a televised stadium-cup tournament rather than a traditional gauntlet-plus-Elite-Four challenge — trainers need a letter of endorsement to enter, must clear eight major-division Gyms in fixed order, and the closing tournament is the Champion Cup at Wyndon Stadium. The league replaced the Elite Four model with the Champion Cup specifically to integrate the Dynamax phenomenon into stadium play, which Chairman Rose framed as Macro Cosmos's contribution to making Galar Gym battles a globally televised spectacle. The Galar Gym Leaders themselves function in-canon as professional celebrity athletes — Bulbapedia and Pokemon.com both characterize them as signed competitive trainers whose gym-leader work is one chapter of a broader media career, with appearances at promotional events, sponsorship by Macro Cosmos's marketing arm, and active fan-club structures (Melony, for example, is publicly listed as the no. 1 fan-club member of her own son Gordie, who heads the rival Sword-version Circhester Gym). This is Pokemon canon's most explicitly corporate-sponsored, mass-spectator framing of competitive battling — and BBL's narrative-first / labor-coded brand voice has an obvious foothold here.
proposed_md_body.what_this_node_anchors: Card art and flavor text that depicts the Galar Pokemon League as an *institution*: its Gym Leaders at their stadium posts (Allister at Stow-on-Side, Melony at Circhester, Kabu at Motostoke, plus the vision-pending Bea / Opal / Nessa / Milo / Marnie cards already in corpus), the league's institutional iconography (gym crests in stadium backdrops, Pokemon League uniform Supporter-card framing, Macro Cosmos signage where it appears), and the canonical thesis the league embodies — that competitive Pokemon battling in Galar is a sponsored televised industry rather than a folk practice. The Galar Gym Challenge is the BBL corpus's strongest Pokemon-side anchor for the apparatus-of-spectacle / labor-as-televised-competition register: any Galar-themed bundle on the gym-leader-as-celebrity-athlete framing, the corporate-conglomerate-as-league-sponsor framing, or the Champion-Cup-as-stadium-cup-tournament framing indexes here.
proposed_md_body.members_in_corpus_currently_enriched: Allister (Vivid Voltage no. 146/185, Ghost-type, Stow-on-Side, Shield major-division, take art) — explicit Bulbapedia citation in trivia for the major-division-not-minor framing; Melony (Chilling Reign no. 146/198, Ice-type, Circhester, Shield major-division, take art) — trivia confirms `take` as the canonical Game Freak character designer for the SWSH Gym Leaders, not a TCG-only illustrator; Kabu (Champion's Path no. 55/73, Fire-type, Motostoke, both-versions major-division, take art) — vision pass with galar-gym-challenge hub tag, IP verified.
proposed_md_body.members_in_corpus_pending_vision: Five additional Galar Gym Leader Supporter cards are already in inventory as vision-stubs with empty tags_hub: Bea (Vivid Voltage 147, Fighting-type, Stow-on-Side Sword-version, take art) — Allister's canonical version-pair foil; Opal (Vivid Voltage 158, Fairy-type, Ballonlea Shield major-division, take art); Nessa (Vivid Voltage 157, Water-type, Hulbury major-division, take art); Milo (Rebel Clash 161, Grass-type, Turffield major-division, take art) — the first Gym Leader in the league progression; Marnie (Sword & Shield Base Set 169, Dark-type, Spikemuth minor-division-elevated-by-rebellion, holofoil, art_match_confidence: low — flagged for parent triage, see Caveats). All five carry `take` artist credit (matching the canonical Game Freak Gym Leader designer chain) and slot cleanly into this node's appears_on once their vision passes land. Mr. Nodeley flags this surplus for the parent's wave-planning visibility — five future cards already exist to attach.
proposed_md_body.the_functional_ideology: The Galar Gym Challenge's most BBL-relevant property is that its institutional logic IS the thesis. Where prior-generation Pokemon leagues organized around regional-craft folkways (Kanto: the eight-gym-trial gauntlet rooted in the Pokemon League as referee-body; Hoenn / Sinnoh / Unova: variations on the same trial-plus-Elite-Four model), the Galar League organized around the *televisability of competitive battling*: a corporate-sponsored stadium-cup tournament where Gym Leaders are signed celebrity athletes, the Dynamax phenomenon was engineered into the league specifically to make matches visually broadcastable at scale, and the conglomerate sponsor (Macro Cosmos under Chairman Rose) provides the league's operational backbone. The league's signature Gym Leader cohort embodies the same shift in vocabulary: Kabu is the disciplined veteran-coach archetype shown in his stadium-arena posture (the Motostoke crest hangs prominently behind him in the card art); Melony is the warm-elder character whose canon includes a publicly-feuding gym-leader son (Gordie) whose split with her is part of the league's televised story; Allister is the shy-introvert-with-power archetype shown against a near-studio backdrop with twin floating ghost-orbs as iconographic flanks, the literal *publicity-shot* visual register. The Sword-Shield version-asymmetric Gym pairings (Allister/Bea at Stow-on-Side, Melony/Gordie at Circhester) function as the league's structural feature, not a bug — version-exclusivity is the league formalizing two parallel championship brackets within one televised season. This is what BBL's bundle copy can lean on without inventing canon: the league is, in-canon, a designer-confirmed apparatus-of-spectacle dressed as a regional sporting tradition.
proposed_md_body.canonical_sources_list_basis: Bulbapedia: Galar League / Gym Challenge / individual Gym Leader pages (Allister, Bea, Melony, Gordie, Kabu, Milo, Opal, Nessa, Marnie, Raihan, Piers, Leon); Pokemon.com: official Galar character pages and 'Top Cards for the Competition in Chilling Reign'-style strategy articles that confirm Gym Leader competitive identities; PokemonTCG.io API: per-card metadata for the SWSH-era Supporter cards confirming `take` artist credit chain; Pokemon Sword & Shield (Game Freak, 2019) primary canon; TheGamer / Pokemon Fandom Wiki / Pojo coverage as journalism-tier secondary sources for the canonical family-feud and version-asymmetry lore.
caveats: **Marnie is a minor-division Gym Leader, not a major-division one — yet.** Per Bulbapedia, Marnie is the Spikemuth Dark-type Gym Leader, and Spikemuth's gym is a *minor* league gym in the base Sword & Shield campaign (bypassable / outside the eight mandatory major-division slots). However, post-game and DLC content elevates Marnie to a Champion-tier challenger and her gym is reframed within the league's broader structure. For purposes of this node's appears_on, Marnie wires here as a Galar Pokemon League Gym Leader broadly; if a future split into `galar-gym-challenge-major-division` vs `galar-gym-challenge-minor-division` sub-nodes becomes warranted, Marnie moves to the minor side and Piers (her brother, who initially holds the Spikemuth slot in the main game) would belong to the same minor-division sub-node alongside her. This is a known structural ambiguity, flagged here for future sub-node planning; for now the parent node holds them all.

**Peonia (Chilling Reign no. 149/198) is NOT a Gym Leader and does NOT belong here.** Peonia is a Crown Tundra civilian NPC (daughter of Peony, niece of Chairman Rose). She belongs to a *separate* Macro Cosmos family-axis cluster — Peony / Rose / Peonia — that overlaps with this node thematically (all three are corporate-aristocratic NPCs in the Galar setting) but is structurally distinct (none of them are Gym Leaders; they're the conglomerate's family). If a future `macro-cosmos-family` character node is warranted, Peonia anchors there. Surface-resemblance trap flagged here so future passes don't conflate corporate-NPC-in-Galar with Gym-Leader-in-Galar.

**Chairman Rose / Oleana / Macro Cosmos itself are not (yet) corpus members.** The league's sponsoring conglomerate has its own villain-arc through Chairman Rose's eternatus-summoning final-act betrayal of the league. No Rose, Oleana, or Macro-Cosmos-specific cards are in current inventory. If those cards arrive, a sibling `macro-cosmos` character node (faction-collective parallel to Endriders) would anchor the corporate-conglomerate side of the league's institutional structure; this node would related_characters: into it. For now the conglomerate is named in the body but doesn't have its own anchor.

**Leon (the reigning Galar Champion at the start of the Sword & Shield story) is not in current corpus.** Leon is the league's headline athlete — Galar's undefeated Champion — and any future Leon card would anchor here as the championship-tier endpoint of the Gym Challenge progression. The Champion-Cup-tournament structure of the league is named in the body but the Champion himself is currently an off-corpus reference. If a Leon card lands (or if his post-game Master Tournament expansion characters do — Marnie, Raihan, Piers in their Champion-tier appearances), the appears_on list extends naturally.

**Version-asymmetric Gym pairs are a designer feature, not bookkeeping noise.** Allister (Shield, Ghost) and Bea (Sword, Fighting) share the Stow-on-Side major slot; Melony (Shield, Ice) and Gordie (Sword, Rock) share the Circhester major slot. Both pairs are designer-coordinated as version-pair foils — the league formalizes two parallel championship brackets within a single televised season, and the gym-leader-as-celebrity-athlete framing extends to the cross-version rivalry storylines (Gordie publicly rejecting Melony's Ice specialty in favor of Rock-type is documented league lore). Cards depicting version-paired leaders edge to this node together; the asymmetry is the institutional feature.

**The `take` artist chain is canonical for SWSH Gym Leader Supporter cards but not exclusive.** Per the Allister card's trivia, the main-print regular Supporter version (146/185) is illustrated by `take`, but the alternate-rarity prints (Allister Full Art 179, Allister Rainbow Rare 192) are illustrated by *different* artists, and the same is true for Melony (her Full Art 195 and Rainbow Secret 218 are by Taira Akitsu; her Astral Radiance Trainer Gallery TG26 is by nagimiso; her Crown Zenith Galarian Gallery GG64 is by saino misaki). If the corpus acquires those alternate-rarity prints, they all anchor here at the character/institution level — the artist split is at the print-object level, not the character level. The parent's artist resolver (artist_resolve.py) handles the per-print attribution; this node anchors the character across prints.

**Bea's Sword-version trivia framing requires anti-confab discipline at trivia-pass time.** Bea is the Sword-version Stow-on-Side Gym Leader specializing in Fighting-type, and her canonical character beat is karate-school-trained Galarian rival-coach. Her vision-pending card in Vivid Voltage will need triviabot to anchor her as a major-division Gym Leader (matching Allister's parallel anchor, not as a 'fighting-type-character' generic) and to surface the Allister/Bea version-pair structure. Mr. Nodeley flags this here so the eventual triviabot pass on Bea wires her in cleanly.
see_also_section_seeds: [[labor]] — the BBL hub most thematically compatible with the Galar Gym Challenge institutional register. The league's entire organizational structure is a televised-spectacle / sponsored-athletics apparatus where Gym Leaders are signed competitive workers under conglomerate management. Labor-as-celebrity-athletics is the league's signature feature; this is the closest existing hub for Galar-region narrative bundles. The Pokemon-side parallel of the Magic-side Orzhov-Azorius apparatus-of-extraction reading.

[[endriders]] — sibling collective-entity character node from MTG/Aetherdrift; the closest structural precedent in the existing graph. Both nodes anchor a designer-coordinated faction-team-roster within a single source IP where the node IS the institution and the individual cards depict its members. Different universes; same shape.

[[azorius-senate]] / [[orzhov-syndicate]] — sibling faction-institution character nodes from MTG/Ravnica; closest *institutional* (vs roster) precedent. Both nodes anchor a Wizards-canon faction whose institutional logic IS the BBL-relevant thesis. The Galar Gym Challenge is the Pokemon-side institutional anchor for the apparatus-of-spectacle thesis, parallel to how the Orzhov + Azorius nodes anchor the Magic-side apparatus-of-extraction thesis.
[[Bulk Graph Bundler]] (wiki) — the project this character node serves.

future Galar Discrete Lair candidate — a Galar Gym Leader bundle anchored here is a natural Discrete Lair candidate once 4–6 corpus Gym Leader cards are vision-passed (currently 3 fully enriched + 5 vision-pending = 8 total; the bundle-cohesion stack topology will be in place once vision lands on the pending five).

topology_notes: stack — this is a bundle-cohesion stack of three (soon to be eight) corpus cards all converging on a single institutional anchor. Mr. Nodeley appreciates a stack. Once the additional five vision passes land, the stack thickens and a Discrete Lair becomes assemblable.
future_passes_unlocked: Vision passes on the five vision-stub Galar Gym Leader cards (Bea, Opal, Nessa, Milo, Marnie) — all five can wire into this node's appears_on list as their tags_hub lands. Recommend prioritizing Bea next (canonical version-pair foil to Allister; the Stow-on-Side major-slot pair is a designer-coordinated cluster). Milo is the league's first Gym in the progression (Turffield Grass) — bookends the cohort with Marnie's Spikemuth endpoint.

Trivia passes on Kabu, Opal, Bea, Nessa, Milo, Marnie — once landed, this node's appears_on stabilizes at the full eight-member Galar Gym Leader cohort and the canonical-sources list can extend to per-leader Bulbapedia citations.

Future `macro-cosmos` sibling character node (when Chairman Rose / Oleana / Macro-Cosmos-administrative cards arrive in inventory) — anchors the corporate-conglomerate sponsor side of the league. Related-characters edge between this node and the macro-cosmos node would complete the league's institutional axis.

Future `macro-cosmos-family` character node (anchoring Peonia + Peony + Rose) for the Crown Tundra family-axis cluster; separate from both this node and the macro-cosmos faction node above. Peonia is already in corpus; Peony and Rose are not. Wait for at least one more arrival.

Future Galar Discrete Lair (bundle) — anchor here once 4+ Gym Leader cards are vision+trivia-passed. The narrative thesis (Gym Leaders as celebrity athletes in a corporate-sponsored televised league) is buyer-facing-ready and brand-voice-aligned. The pricing lever: 8+ Supporter cards in inventory means qty_in_bundle > 1 is on the table for Allister / Marnie quantities (Allister qty 3, Marnie qty 6 — the bulk-disposal mechanic per the bbl-multi-copy-bundles memory).

Future `galar-gym-challenge-minor-division` sub-node split (if Marnie's pre-DLC minor-Gym framing warrants a separate anchor) — parallel to how the Azorius node flagged a future `azorius-sphinx-jurists` sub-node candidate. Not warranted yet at one minor-division card; trigger when 2+ minor-division Galar Gym cards arrive (Piers would be the second; Marnie's brother shares the Spikemuth slot pre-DLC).

alternatives_considered.candidate: Smoky Lounge // Misty Salon ↔ Moldering Gym // Weight Room 1:1 Room-mechanic mirror at cards/magic-the-gathering/duskmourn-house-of-horror/
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Both are DSK Room cards and both per agent's vision read carry stag-skull / trophy-hall hunter's-manor iconography — the surface case for a 1:1 mirror is real. But the connection is mechanical-cycle membership (the Room keyword as a DSK keyword-cycle) plus shared set-flavor iconography, not narrative cohesion at the per-card level. Room is a designer-coordinated keyword-cycle with 30+ cards in DSK; drawing a 1:1 between two of them would write a duplicate bullet pointing at an absent cycle-center. The right shape here is a future Nodeley pass proposing a `dsk-room-cycle` node (parallel to the fear-of-cycle node) once 3+ Room cards land in corpus and at least one is trivia-passed enough to confirm the keyword-cycle anchor. Defer. Currently 2 Room cards in corpus (Smoky Lounge // Misty Salon, Moldering Gym // Weight Room); waiting on a third anchor card with vision+trivia complete.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Glimmer cycle character/cycle node at cards/_characters/glimmer-cycle.md
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Trivia harvested 5 related Glimmer-typed DSK cards (Inquisitive Glimmer, Glimmerlight, Glimmerburst, Lionheart Glimmer, Acrobatic Cheerleader) as canonical-cycle members, but only ONE — Glimmer Seeker — is confirmed in current inventory. Below the 3+ corpus-member threshold for Mr. Nodeley node-creation. Refuse cleanly. Re-evaluate when 3+ Glimmer cards are in corpus AND at least 2 are trivia-passed enough to confirm cycle membership; current state of one is too thin. Flag for a future scan once another Glimmer card lands.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Nicholas Gregory artist node at cards/_artists/nicholas-gregory.md
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Skycrash trivia confirmed Nicholas Gregory illustrated both Skycrash and Fuel the Flames. Two cards. Below Nodeley 3+ threshold for artist-node creation. The bbl-artists-layer-built memory's first entry (Jenn Ravenna Tran) had 3+ corpus credits AND a printing-fidelity alias-divergence problem that the artist node solved; Gregory currently has neither (2 credits, no known alias-divergence issue). Defer until a third Gregory card lands AND there's an actual artist-resolve.py-relevant alias problem to solve.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Edgelord 1:1 mirror between Allister and Melony on shared `take` illustrator + shared Shield-major-division Gym Leader role
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: The candidate connection is real — same illustrator, same league, same Shield-major-division slot, designer-coordinated character cohort. But it would write a duplicate bullet pointing at the absent league anchor (the very situation that triggers Mr. Nodeley per the spec). Drawing the Allister-Melony 1:1 mirror would also force me to draw an Allister-Kabu 1:1 and a Melony-Kabu 1:1 to complete the triangle, then re-draw all three when Bea / Opal / Nessa / Milo / Marnie land. The Nodeley node short-circuits the cascade — each new card attaches via one frontmatter pointer to the institution instead of N-1 1:1 edges to every prior card in the cohort. Refuse the 1:1; propose the node.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Diversion Specialist correction edge / refusal note
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Trivia explicitly corrected the prior task-profile claim that Diversion Specialist is a 'Duskmourn Survivor creature-type' — it's actually Human Warrior. This is anti-confab discipline holding (and is the kind of correction the trivia pass is *supposed* to surface, not an Edgelord move). No graph operation warranted. Flagged here as the slice's fifth candidate move per the prompt; resolved as a correctness-confirmation, not a graph-write.

layer_proposal_notes.recommendation: wikilintbot already accepts type: character. No code changes required.
layer_proposal_notes.frontmatter_schema_notes: Schema follows the Endriders / Azorius-Senate / Orzhov-Syndicate collective-entity precedents rather than the Nicol-Bolas individual-character precedent. Key decisions: `faction` field describes the institution (Galar Pokemon League, with sponsor and structure cited); `species` field carries the faction-collective descriptive; `ip_resolution_for: galar-gym-challenge` mirrors how nicol-bolas / azorius-senate / orzhov-syndicate use the field as a suspected_ip resolution target.

dual_pipeline_judge_notes.intended_followup: Per slice instructions, this proposal is formatted to slot into the dual-pipeline + bbl-judge convergence test path. Parent dispatches Sonnet-side Edgelord on the same prompt, Judge compares the two outputs, verdicts convergence before applying. Per the bbl-dual-pipeline-judge-pattern memory, this is the canonical pattern for load-bearing structural moves (new layers, node dissolutions, enlightened replacements). A faction-institution character node spanning 3 enriched + 5 vision-stub corpus cards qualifies as load-bearing.
convergence_targets: Both pipelines should converge on the SAME slug (galar-gym-challenge) — the slug is grounded in the canonical Bulbapedia page name 'Gym Challenge' rendered as the region-qualified kebab-case 'galar-gym-challenge', and is already in two corpus cards' tags_hub. If Sonnet picks a different slug (e.g. 'galar-pokemon-league' or 'galar-gym-leaders') the Judge should arbitrate; the tags_hub-grounded slug is the recommended landing.

Both pipelines should converge on the SAME three appears_on entries (Allister Vivid Voltage 146-185, Melony Chilling Reign 146-198, Kabu Champion's Path 55-73). Sonnet may report a different corpus count if its enrichment snapshot differs from Opus's — per the Edgelord spec's model dispatch note, Sonnet was previously seen at half the Opus corpus snapshot. If Sonnet reports 0, 1, or 2 enriched Gym Leaders instead of 3, that's a snapshot-coverage divergence, not a graph-move divergence; the Judge should weight Opus's count in that case.

Both pipelines should converge on the SAME faction framing (Galar Pokemon League, Macro Cosmos sponsorship, televised stadium-cup format, Gym-Leaders-as-celebrity-athletes). The canonical-sources citations are the load-bearing constraint; both pipelines should cite Bulbapedia's Galar League / Gym Challenge / individual Gym Leader pages as primary, Pokemon.com character pages as secondary, PokemonTCG.io as metadata-tier. Anti-confab discipline applies on both sides.

Both pipelines should converge on the SAME hub edge ([[labor]]). The labor hub captures the labor-as-celebrity-athletics framing; both pipelines should land here. If Sonnet proposes [[rebellion]] instead (or in addition), the Judge should arbitrate — Marnie's eventual minor-division-elevated-by-rebellion lore is a rebellion-coded story-beat but it's a future-pass concern, not this node's framing concern.
convergence_failure_modes_to_watch: Sonnet writes a generic 'Galar Region' or 'Pokemon Sword Shield' node instead of the league-institution-specific node. This is too-broad; the right scope is the league as institution, not the region or the source-IP-game.

Sonnet conflates Peonia into the node despite the prompt's explicit exclusion. The Crown Tundra civilian / Gym-Leader distinction is a known surface-resemblance trap. The Judge should flag this and reject the Peonia inclusion if it appears.

Sonnet under-counts the corpus (reports only 2 enriched cards instead of 3 due to corpus-snapshot drift). Acceptable as a snapshot-issue; the Judge should still verdict convergence on the node-proposal itself, with a note to the parent that Opus's snapshot is the canonical appears_on at apply time.

Sonnet proposes the dsk-room-cycle node or the glimmer-cycle node instead of the galar-gym-challenge node (i.e., picks a different one of the five candidate moves). This is a *prioritization* divergence, not a *correctness* divergence — both pipelines may pick valid moves and the Judge arbitrates which is strongest. Mr. Nodeley's prioritization (galar-gym-challenge first, dsk-room-cycle second when 3+ Room cards land) is grounded in: (a) more corpus members already enriched, (b) more vision-stub members waiting to attach, (c) stronger institutional thesis for buyer-facing copy, (d) tags_hub-grounded slug already in two cards' frontmatter.
